source_note: ICRC internal — Claude API + web search (uavarta.org, mezha.net, criticalthreats.org/ISW, pravda-news.com, kyivpost.com, southfront.press/maps, cert.gov.ua via thehackernews.com/therecord.media, themilitant.com, reliefweb.int, wikipedia.org/2025_Sumy_offensive, halo trust, DRC). Field validation required before operational use.

incidents.description: Russian forces carried out 90+ attacks on 25 settlements across 11 communities; 4 civilians injured (01–02 Mar).
Russian drone struck a 3-storey residential building in Sumy city centre evening of 2 March; no initial casualties reported.
Russian forces continued offensive operations in northern Sumy Oblast; attacks reported near Varachyne and Pokrovka on 28 Feb–1 Mar.
incidents.source: ISW/CriticalThreats — 01/03/2026; Wikipedia/2025 Sumy Offensive — updated Mar 2026

incidents.description: Russian forces launched ~130 attacks on 47 settlements across 19 communities (6–7 Mar); 4 civilians injured by drone strikes in Sumy, Berezivka, Trostianets communities.
Enemy UAV strike in Sumy community injured a 36-year-old woman and a 59-year-old man; morning of 7 March.
62-year-old civilian injured in an enemy drone strike in Trostianets community, as reported morning 7 March.
Russian mass strike on energy facilities in Chernihiv border areas; railway infrastructure in Snovsk also targeted; widespread power outages reported (7 Mar).
Russian FPV drone struck gymnasium roof in Semenivka community on 6 March; border communities Novhorod-Siverskyi, Semenivka, Snovsk also shelled.
Russian forces attacked 3 Chernihiv border communities on 6 Mar: mortar (8 exp.), artillery (4 exp.), Molniia UAV (1), FPV drones (14 exp.) plus dropped ordnance.
Russian mass drone/missile strike package targeted Kyiv and 15 other oblasts night 6–7 Mar; debris landed Solomyanskyi/Svyatoshynskyi; air-raid active.
Fires recorded at private property Holosiivskyi district; 2-storey residential house caught fire Pecherskyi district from Shahed drone debris.
incidents.source: Kyiv Independent (combined missile/drone attack article) — 06/03/2026; KMVA Tkachenko statement

incidents.description: Russian strikes targeted CHPP-5 and CHPP-6 in Kyiv region night of 6–7 Mar; energy infrastructure hit causing power disruptions across oblast.
Vodafone base station providing mobile comms in Esman destroyed by Russian shelling 6 Mar; emergency repairs impossible due to security situation.
Active Russian offensive operations and FPV drone saturation in border raions severely restrict humanitarian movement; 15th Border Detachment engaged throughout March.

incidents.cible: Civilians / humanitarian operators on rural routes
incidents.description: Active Russian offensive incursion zones in north Sumy confirmed mined; landmine/UXO contamination on border roads and forest belts poses acute hazard to field staff.
incidents.source: HALO Trust Ukraine programme — active; Landmine Monitor 2024 / HI.org (Sumy/Chernihiv listed); Wikipedia/2025 Sumy Offensive

incidents.cible: Returnee civilians / humanitarian staff on secondary roads
incidents.description: Residual UXO/ERW contamination in Bucha/Irpin/Hostomel district: ongoing demining operations (HALO Trust active). Risk to ICRC vehicles on non-surveyed routes.
incidents.source: HALO Trust Ukraine (working in Kyiv Oblast, confirmed 2026); DRC demining Rozvazhiv/Kyiv Oblast report

incidents.description: Residual UXO/ERW contamination from 2022 occupation and ongoing shelling across Chernihiv Oblast. Landmine Monitor 2024 lists Chernihiv among most affected oblasts.
incidents.source: Landmine Monitor 2024 (HI.org); HALO Trust Ukraine — active Chernihiv ops

incidents.description: GPS jamming/spoofing endemic in Kyiv and surroundings during mass drone attacks; disrupts navigation for field teams and air defence coordination.
incidents.source: Kyiv Post EW analysis — Mar 2026; RAND EW report Nov 2025; GeoGarage GPS jamming analysis Mar 2026

incidents.description: 68 shellings + 3 airstrikes (8 KABs) in Sumy Northern Sloboda direction on 6–7 Mar; access authorisations for border hromadas effectively suspended by military.

incidents.cible: Public order / ICRC freedom of movement
incidents.description: Over 3,000 women marched central Kyiv 8 Mar (International Women's Day); first such march since full-scale invasion; peacefully organised, no violence reported.

incidents.cible: NGOs / medical centres / state organisations (IT systems)
incidents.description: Pro-Russian group UAC-0247 ran phishing campaign Mar–Apr 2026 targeting healthcare, local govt using fake 'humanitarian assistance' emails; CERT-UA advisory issued.

incidents.cible: NGOs / state organisations / ICRC-adjacent digital infrastructure
incidents.description: Russian-linked malware campaign (BadPaw/MeowMeow) used phishing email disguised as Ukrainian border checkpoint permit to target organisations (reported ~4 Mar).

incidents.cible: ICRC field teams / all civilian vehicles
incidents.description: Martial law and military restrictions in effect across Sumy border raions; movements restricted by curfew and military zone designations throughout March 2026.
Continuous daily bombardment rhythm across Sumy Oblast (90+ attacks/24h cycle) generates acute chronic stress and operational fatigue risk for all field staff.
Russian drones targeted Kyiv Oblast energy infrastructure 6–7 Mar; Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas types confirmed; debris impacts in suburban areas noted.

sra_updates.rationale: 3+ verified incidents (INC-014, INC-019, INC-023) confirm near-complete denial of humanitarian access to Seredyna-Buda, Shalyhyne, Yunakivka and Krasnopillia border hromadas. Russian offensive operations, 68+ shellings/24h, KAB airstrikes, and active military zone designations have suspended all non-military movement authorisations in northern Sumy raions throughout the reporting period.

3+ verified cyber incidents in the period (INC-021, INC-022, and background CERT-UA UAC-0247 campaign spiking Mar–Apr 2026) confirm sustained, targeted phishing operations against healthcare, NGOs, and government entities. UAC-0247 specifically uses fake 'humanitarian assistance' lures, posing direct and specific risk to ICRC digital infrastructure and staff email accounts.

March 2026 saw record Shahed UAV launch volumes (avg. 208/day per ISIS analysis) accompanied by intensive Russian EW/GPS jamming over Kyiv and Sumy. GPS spoofing documented as endemic to the Ukrainian battlefield. During the reporting week, mass attacks on 6–7 Mar with 211 drones over Kyiv region confirmed; ICRC vehicles using GPS navigation face elevated risk of positional loss during air-raid events.
